fields.description:
## Why
Provision the auto-lander bot's Gerrit credential and the access right it needs to rebase changes on behalf of their uploader — reusing the project's existing ACL machinery, without a redundant grant or an IAM change. Split out of S5 (`perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon`).

## Files
- `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` — EDIT: add `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` to the existing `local.rebar_secret_params` set (the `for_each` `aws_ssm_parameter` resource with `ignore_changes = [value]` + CHANGEME sentinel), NOT a standalone resource.
- `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` — EDIT: add the mapping `autolander-gerrit-token → AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`, materialized into the SAME shared `.env` (distinct env-var from the review-bot's `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN`).
- `infra/gerrit/project.config` + `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` — EDIT: grant the bot the **Rebase** access right (and the rebase-on-behalf-of-uploader form) on the `rebar` project's `refs/heads/*`. This lives in the **rebar project's own** `refs/meta/config` (managed here), NOT `All-Projects`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Credential:** the Gerrit HTTP token is an SSM **SecureString** at `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` — inside the existing `parameter/rebar/prod/*` prefix the EC2 instance-role IAM policy (`iam.tf` `ssm_params_read`) already grants, so **NO IAM change** (`iam.tf` read-only/unchanged; a `/rebar/autolander/*` path would fall outside the grant). Added via the `ssm.tf` `for_each` set; fetched by `fetch-secrets.sh` into the shared `.env` as `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`; never inlined.
- [ ] **Submit is already covered — do NOT add a submit grant.** `RebarBotNava` is already in the `Contributors` group (`setup-project.sh` default `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS`), which holds `Submit` on `refs/heads/*`. An All-Projects submit grant would be **eclipsed** anyway: `project.config` sets `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit`, so Gerrit ignores inherited submit for that ref. AC = *verify* `RebarBotNava ∈ Contributors` (so `POST /submit` does not 403); no new submit ACL.
- [ ] **Rebase-on-behalf access right (corrected mechanism):** to call `rebase`/`rebase:chain` with `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true`, the bot needs the **`Rebase`** access right (a real per-ref ACL in Gerrit, confirmed in this instance's `access-control.html`) on the `rebar` project's `refs/heads/*` — NOT a global `[capability]` and NOT the project-level `[submit] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` submit-strategy setting (those are different things). It is added to the **rebar project's** `refs/meta/config` via `setup-project.sh`/`project.config`. **Verify at implementation** the exact access-right name/form for on-behalf-of-uploader rebase against this instance's `access-control.html` (Gerrit-version-specific), then encode it in `project.config`.
- [ ] **Tests:** `setup-project.sh` remains idempotent (re-run is a no-op); a smoke check confirms the token materializes into `.env`, `RebarBotNava` can authenticate, rebase-on-behalf a test change (uploader preserved), and submit it — all against the live instance.
